places.name: Nilometer
places.description: An ancient instrument used to measure the waters of the Nile so Egyptian farmers would know whether to expect famine or flood.

places.name: Monastery of Saint Simon
places.description: The massive cave church of Cairo's Zabbaleen community is one of the largest Christian churches in the Middle East.

places.name: Al-Andalus Garden
places.description: A preserved oasis with traditional Andalusian-style architecture and beautiful views of the Nile.

places.name: Cairo's City of the Dead
places.description: In Egypt's expanding metro area, many of the neighbors are dead.

places.name: The Hanging Church
places.description: Standing atop 2nd-century Roman ruins, this church is one of the oldest and most iconic in Old Cairo.

places.name: The Cairo Genizah
places.description: Hundreds of thousands of ancient documents offer a unique glimpse into Jewish life in Cairo.

places.name: The Aquarium Grotto Garden
places.description: A strange aquarium with stuffed fish, and almost no water for them to swim in.
